statistics.name: assists
statistics.description: The number of times a player who passes the ball to a teammate in a way that leads to a score by field goal, meaning that he or she was "assisting" in the basket. There is some judgment involved in deciding whether a passer should be credited with an assist.

articles.headline: Igor Kokoskov joins Project B as head of player development
articles.description: Longtime NBA assistant Igor Kokoskov has joined the startup men's and women's basketball league Project B as its head of player development, the organization announced Thursday.

articles.headline: Ranking the WNBA's top 10 rim protectors of all time
articles.description: A'ja Wilson, Brittney Griner and Breanna Stewart already rank among the best shot blockers, rebounders and post presences.

articles.headline: Chicago hosts Los Angeles after Burrell's 24-point outing
articles.description: Los Angeles Sparks (10-13, 5-7 Western Conference) at Chicago Sky (8-16, 1-7 Eastern Conference)

articles.headline: Fever's Caitlin Clark sounds off on non-call in loss to Valkyries
articles.description: Caitlin Clark was frustrated by a non-call midway through the second quarter Wednesday night and sounded off after the Fever's 88-75 loss to the Valkyries.

images.name: Caitlin Clark [600x400]
images.caption: "The ref can't miss that ... then I have to play with a contusion in my leg the rest of the game," Caitlin Clark said postgame. "It's ridiculous. ... [The ref] said I initiated the contact. Which is fine, but you can't knee me in the leg. Knock me over."

images.name: Caitlin Clark takes hard fall after contact on drive, comes up limping
images.caption: Caitlin Clark takes hard fall after contact on drive, comes up limping

articles.headline: Valkyries beat the Fever 88-75 for 8th straight victory
articles.description: — Gabby Williams scored 16 points, Kaitlyn Chen had 14 points, all in the second half, and the Golden State Valkyries beat the Indiana Fever 88-75 on Wednesday night for the second-year franchise's record eighth straight victory.

articles.headline: Full list of every ESPYS 2026 award winner
articles.description: From Jalen Brunson to A'ja Wilson, see which athletes won at the 2026 ESPYS.

images.name: ESPYS Trophy [1296x729]
images.caption: First broadcast in 1993, The ESPYS help raise funds and awareness for the V Foundation for Cancer Research. ESPN has helped raise over $292 million for the V Foundation in the past 33 years.

images.name: Ali Sports Humanitarian Award: Stephen and Ayesha Curry
images.caption: Ali Sports Humanitarian Award: Four-time NBA champion and two-time MVP Stephen Curry and entrepreneur, host and two-time New York Times best-selling author Ayesha Curry.

articles.headline: Teresa Weatherspoon, Cynthia Cooper pick WNBA All-Star teams
articles.description: Teresa Weatherspoon bragged about the competitiveness of her team as the 2026 WNBA All-Star draft concluded Wednesday. Her All-Star general manager counterpart Cynthia Cooper said in response: "But can you score enough points?"

images.name: Cynthia Cooper, Teresa Weatherspoon excited about their ASG rosters
images.caption: Cynthia Cooper, Teresa Weatherspoon excited about their ASG rosters

articles.headline: Lynx beat the Sparks 96-87 behind Kayla McBride's 5th straight 20-point game
articles.description: — Kayla McBride scored 24 for her fifth straight 20-point performance, Courtney Williams added 19 points, and the Minnesota Lynx beat the Los Angeles Sparks 96-87 on Wednesday for their fourth straight victory.

articles.headline: Azura Stevens scores 18 of her 20 points in the 1st half and the Sky beat the Storm 95-90
articles.description: — Azura Stevens scored 18 of her 20 points in the first half, Sydney Taylor added 17 points, and the Chicago Sky beat the Seattle Storm 95-90 on Wednesday.
